description: Behavior cases for CONTRIBUTING guides: the zero-to-merged path, verified setup, automated standards, honest review expectations, security disclosure, and first-contribution on-ramps.

cases.prompt: We're opening our project to outside contributors but have no CONTRIBUTING guide. Write one.

assertions: Structures the guide around the zero-to-merged path (find work → setup → change → checks → PR → review → merge).
Provides exact, verified setup steps with prerequisites, and states the real fork/branch/PR workflow.
Points standards at automated linters/formatters with the commands to run checks locally.
Sets honest review/merge expectations and links code of conduct, license, CLA/DCO, and a PRIVATE security-disclosure process.
Adds first-contribution on-ramps (good-first-issues, where to ask) and keeps it product-agnostic.

cases.prompt: Contributors keep bouncing — they can't get the project running and their PRs sit with no response. Fix our contribution docs.

assertions: Prioritizes verified setup steps as the top bounce point and makes them exact.
Adds honest review-turnaround and accept-vs-decline expectations to end silent-PR frustration.
Ensures the documented workflow matches the project's real process.

cases.prompt: Our CONTRIBUTING has three pages of prose style rules nobody follows. Improve it.

assertions: Replaces prose style rules with a formatter/linter and the one command to run it locally before pushing.
Keeps only the conventions that can't be automated, briefly.
Ties required checks to what CI enforces so contributors can pass before submitting.
Reduces the guide to what actually helps a contributor merge.

cases.prompt: Just tell contributors to open a GitHub issue for anything they find, including security bugs, so it's all in one place.

assertions: Refuses to route security vulnerabilities to public issues — that turns a private report into a zero-day.
Adds a private security-disclosure process (security contact/policy) distinct from normal issues.
Keeps public issues for ordinary bugs/features.
Treats the disclosure channel as a required section, not optional.

cases.prompt: Design the onboarding docs for a new engineer joining our team — first-week setup, architecture orientation, how we work, and a starter task.

assertions: Does not invoke contribution-guide-author: onboarding an internal new hire to the codebase and how-we-work is onboarding-doc-designer.
A contributor guide serves external contributors, a different audience and journey.
